original_context: Claim ELISA-equivalent performance from buffer calibration.
correction: Include whole-blood matrix spike recovery controls and reference ELISA comparison before claiming ELISA-equivalent sensitivity.
reason: Buffer-only calibration does not prove whole-blood performance.
derived_rule: For CRP whole-blood biosensor validation, include matrix-effect controls and compare against a reference ELISA; do not claim ELISA-equivalent performance from buffer-only calibration.
applicable_rule: For CRP whole-blood biosensor validation, include matrix-effect controls and compare against a reference ELISA; do not claim ELISA-equivalent performance from buffer-only calibration.

experiment_type: in vivo mouse probiotic supplementation study
correction: Use a justified group size, randomize across cages, and account for cage effects; avoid underpowered n=3 designs.
reason: Mouse permeability studies have substantial biological and cage-level variance.
derived_rule: For C57BL/6 FITC-dextran intestinal permeability studies, include randomization, cage-effect awareness, and a justified group size; avoid underpowered n=3 designs.
applicable_rule: For C57BL/6 FITC-dextran intestinal permeability studies, include randomization, cage-effect awareness, and a justified group size; avoid underpowered n=3 designs.

original_context: Compare trehalose without DMSO standard or cell QC.
correction: Include DMSO standard control, post-thaw recovery timepoint, cell-line authentication, and mycoplasma testing.
reason: Cell-line quality and standard control are necessary to interpret cryopreservation comparisons.
derived_rule: For HeLa cryopreservation comparisons, include DMSO standard control, post-thaw recovery timepoint, cell-line authentication, and mycoplasma testing.
applicable_rule: For HeLa cryopreservation comparisons, include DMSO standard control, post-thaw recovery timepoint, cell-line authentication, and mycoplasma testing.

correction: Normalize acetate rate to reactor volume, electrode area, and biomass where possible, and verify cathode potential against SHE.
reason: Benchmarking is misleading without normalization and potential calibration.
derived_rule: For Sporomusa ovata bioelectrochemical acetate production, normalize acetate rate to reactor volume, electrode area, and biomass where possible, and verify cathode potential against SHE.
applicable_rule: For Sporomusa ovata bioelectrochemical acetate production, normalize acetate rate to reactor volume, electrode area, and biomass where possible, and verify cathode potential against SHE.

hypothesis: Synthesis of a novel yolk-shell nanoparticle architecture comprising a 1.0 micron ellipsoidal hematite alpha-Fe2O3 core and a 100 nm amorphous silica SiO2 shell, separated by a discrete 20 nm interstitial aqueous gap. The protocol must utilize a sacrificial organic interlayer like polystyrene or tpm to define the void space. The final plan must ensure the structural integrity of the silica shell remains intact during the removal of the sacrificial layer, resulting in a core that is physically detached from the shell interior.

parsed_hypothesis.intervention: Use of sacrificial organic interlayer like polystyrene or tpm
parsed_hypothesis.primary_outcome: Successful synthesis of yolk-shell nanoparticle with intact silica shell and detached core
parsed_hypothesis.mechanism: Utilization of sacrificial organic interlayer to define void space and ensure structural integrity during removal
key_measurements: Physical detachment of core from shell interior
safety_flags: Chemical hazards from handling polystyrene or tpm
Chemical hazards from handling hematite and silica nanoparticles

correction: I want to do the reaction with 6M NaOH and 2M FeCl3 at 100°C for 7 days in the oven, to get 1.2um hematite cubes
derived_rule: For synthesizing hematite particles in nanotechnology experiments, consider using 6M NaOH and 2M FeCl3 at 100°C for 7 days to obtain 1.2 micron hematite cubes, as this method may simplify the reaction process.
applicable_rule: For synthesizing hematite particles in nanotechnology experiments, consider using 6M NaOH and 2M FeCl3 at 100°C for 7 days to obtain 1.2 micron hematite cubes, as this method may simplify the reaction process.

correction: Always order DMSO from Sigma-Aldrich for our lab — that is our standing supplier policy.
reason: Lab policy locked to Sigma for solvents.
derived_rule: The lab has a standing supplier policy to order DMSO from Sigma-Aldrich. This policy applies to all relevant experiments in the lab.
applicable_rule: Always order DMSO from Sigma-Aldrich as per lab supplier policy.

correction: Authenticate cell lines by STR every 6 months for any cell-culture experiment.
derived_rule: For any cell-culture experiment, it is important to authenticate cell lines by STR every 6 months to maintain experimental rigour. This ensures the reliability and validity of the results obtained from cell biology experiments.
applicable_rule: Authenticate cell lines by STR every 6 months for any cell-culture experiment.
